qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in SendSnapshot RPC allows arbitrary file write via unsanitized chunk filename
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a path traversal vulnerability in kv_pebble.go AddChunk due to missing sanitization of SnapshotChunk.Name. The advisory describes a TLS CA certificate chain validation failure in common/security/tls.go trustedCertPool() which only parses the first PEM block. The root causes, vulnerable code locations, and vulnerability classes (CWE-22 path traversal vs. CWE-295 improper certificate validation) are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a TLS CA certificate chain validation failure in common/security/tls.go where trustedCertPool() only parses the first PEM block from multi-certificate CA bundles (CWE-295). The sole qualifying finding describes a path traversal in kv_pebble.go where unsanitized SnapshotChunk.Name reaches os.OpenFile via filepath.Join (CWE-22). These are different vulnerabilities in different code with different root causes.
evidence: Advisory root cause: trustedCertPool() in common/security/tls.go calls pem.Decode() only once, discarding additional PEM certificates in CA bundle files.
Finding root cause: AddChunk in kv_pebble.go:809 passes unsanitized SnapshotChunk.Name to os.OpenFile via filepath.Join, enabling path traversal.
Different vulnerable code files: tls.go vs kv_pebble.go, follower_controller.go, internal_rpc_server.go.
Different CWE classes: CWE-295 (Improper Certificate Validation) vs CWE-22 (Path Traversal).
No overlap in function, endpoint, parser state, sanitizer bypass, or security check between the advisory and the finding.
